articles.title: What are the main events for today?
articles.expand: EUROPEAN SESSION In the European session, the only highlight is the Swiss CPI report. Headline CPI Y/Y is expected to rise to 0.6% vs 0.3% in the prior month due to higher energy prices. The data isn't going to change anything for the SNB though as the central bank has all the reasons to look through the energy supply shock. AMERICAN SESSION In the...

articles.title: USD/JPY: Intervention and weak core CPI cap Yen support – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur reports that Japanese authorities appear to be intervening around USD/JPY 157 after the pair briefly hit 160.72. Tokyo inflation data show headline gains driven solely by energy, while core inflation has fallen to a one‑year low.

articles.title: Crude Oil Prices Surge as Iran Tensions Heat Up
articles.expand: June WTI crude oil (CLM26 ) on Monday closed up +4.48 (+4.39%), and June RBOB gasoline (RBM26 ) closed up +0.1430 (+3.98%). Crude oil and gasoline prices recovered from overnight losses and moved sharply higher on signs of heightened tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. Crude prices shot higher Monday...

articles.title: Bullock Speech: RBA Governor speaks on interest rate outlook at the press conference
articles.expand: Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) Governor Michele Bullock is speaking at the press conference, explaining the reasons behind raising the benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points (bps) to 4.35% after the May monetary policy meeting.

articles.title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Weakens below 1.3550, but maintains bullish bias above key averages
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair loses ground to near 1.3520 during the early European session on Tuesday.

articles.title: Dollar Rises as US-Iran Ceasefire Comes Under Strain, Markets Brace for Escalation Without Panic
articles.expand: Dollar is rising as risk aversion creeps back into global markets as the US–Iran ceasefire comes under increasing strain. Developments over the past 48 hours suggest the fragile truce is under mounting pressure, raising the risk of renewed escalation. However, price action indicates investors are bracing for further conflict rather than pricing a f...

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD attracts some sellers for the third straight day
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair trades with a negative bias for the third straight day on Tuesday, though it lacks follow-through selling and holds above the 1.3500 psychological mark during the Asian session.

articles.title: Gold struggles to extend recovery from one-month low as hawkish Fed bets support USD
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) clings to modest recovery gains through the Asian session on Monday, albeit it lacks bullish conviction and remains close to an over one-month low, around the $4,500 mark, touched the previous day.

articles.title: AUD/JPY trims losses near 112.50 as RBA hikes official cash rate to 4.35%
articles.expand: The AUD/JPY cross pares losses near 112.65 during the early European trading hours on Tuesday. The Australian Dollar (AUD) edges slightly higher after the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) interest rate decision.

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Bulls hesitant above 1.3600 as Oil prices counter USD strength
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair struggles to capitalize on a two-day-old recovery move from the 1.3550 area, or its lowest level since March 10, and oscillates in a range during the Asian session on Tuesday. Spot prices currently trade around the 1.3620 area amid a combination of diverging forces.

articles.title: RBA Hikes Interest Rate to 4.35%, Signals It’s Not Done Yet
articles.expand: The RBA has made one thing clear: it is not done yet. By lifting the cash rate to 4.35%, with the decision backed by an 8–1 majority, and signaling a path toward 4.7% by the end of 2026, the central bank is shifting decisively into a higher-for-longer stance as inflation proves more persistent than expected. articles.title: Australian Dollar remains subdued despite RBA rate hike
articles.expand: AUD/USD holds losses for the second consecutive day, hovering around 0.7160 during the Asian hours on Tuesday. The Australian Dollar (AUD) moves little following the release of the Reserve Bank of Australia’s (RBA) policy decision.

articles.title: AUD/USD Analysis for May 5, 2026: Hawkish RBA Meets a Stubborn Ceiling
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of Australia raised rates for the third time in 2026, a move that could keep AUD supported much longer. But will other macro factors complicate the near-term picture?

articles.title: Statement by the Monetary Policy Board: Monetary Policy Decision
articles.expand: At its meeting today, the Board decided to increase the cash rate target by 25 basis points to 4.35 per cent.

articles.title: USD/CHF inches higher to near 0.7850 ahead of Swiss CPI data
articles.expand: USD/CHF holds ground for the third consecutive day, trading around 0.7840 during the Asian hours on Tuesday. The Swiss Federal Statistical Office is set to release the April Consumer Price Index (CPI) data later in the day.

articles.title: Heads up: RBA monetary policy decision set for the bottom of the hour
articles.expand: In bringing the cash rate back to 4.35%, the RBA will have essentially reversed its brief easing cycle in 2025. This will then bring interest rates back to the recent peak levels seen during 2024, which followed from rate hikes during 2022 to 2023. So much for being done with the battle against inflation, eh? Any further rate hikes after today will...

articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair edges lower to near 1.3620 during the early Asian trading hours on Tuesday. Escalating tensions in the Middle East after the United Arab Emirates (UAE) reports missiles and drone threats from Iran underpin the commodity-linked Canadian Dollar (CAD).

articles.title: USD/JPY churns near 157.00 after the BoJ's FX intervention shock
articles.expand: USD/JPY traded essentially flat on Monday, settling close to 157.20 after a choppy session that opened with an early-Asia gap lower toward 156.20 before staging a steady recovery through European and US trade.

articles.title: IMF says worst-case global economy warning is now the working assumption - oil shock hit
articles.expand: IMF chief Georgieva has abandoned the fund's mild slowdown forecast, warning that if the Middle East war continues into 2027 with oil at $125 a barrel, a "much worse" global economic outcome must be expected. Summary: The IMF's previous baseline forecast of a minor global growth slowdown and modest price increases from the Middle East conflict has ...

articles.expand: XAU/USD, XAG/USD Outlook: Precious metals decline amid escalating Middle East tensions and surging inflation worries. articles.expand: THE war with Iran has boosted prices of globally traded natural gas by throttling exports from the Gulf. In West Texas, gas is so abundant that some producers must pay to have it taken away.

articles.title: Eurozone May Sentix investor confidence -16.4 vs -21.0 expected
articles.expand: Prior -19.2 Euro area investor morale surprisingly picks up in May, indicating that investors do not expect a further escalation to the US-Iran conflict. There was one notable exception though, being Germany, with the index there falling further to -30.9 from -27.7 in April. articles.title: Eurozone PMI Manufacturing at Multi-Year High as War Triggers Record Cost Pressures
articles.expand: Eurozone Manufacturing PMI was finalized at 52.2 in April, rising from March’s 51.6 and marking a 47-month high, signaling a broad-based expansion across the bloc. Notably, all eight countries covered in the survey registered readings above the 50 threshold for the first time since June 2022. The improvement was also reflected in output, with...

articles.title: RBNZ board member Gai says Hormuz shock does not warrant reflexive rate hikes
articles.expand: RBNZ board member Professor Prasanna Gai says the Hormuz supply shock does not imply reflexive tightening, but has raised the neutral rate. Pre-emptive hikes are only warranted when synchronisation is high.
